curation_notes: The advisory references commit a37f594 (test-only, added after the fix) and the v1.2.1...v1.2.2 release comparison. The actual security fix is commit 64dd950 ('fix idempotence'), which replaces direct charAt() lookups with new unescapedChar()/unescapedCharRev() helper methods in the sanitizeString method to properly decode escape sequences when checking for dangerous HTML/XML patterns (<script, <!--, -->, ]]>). Only one non-test source file was modified: JsonSanitizer.java. Pre-assigned as xxe from CWE-611, but the vulnerability is about injecting HTML/XML control sequences (including closing script tags) into embedding documents by bypassing output encoding via escape sequences — reclassified to xss.
